LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SERGIO IANTORNO AND GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE VARIOUS CAUSES OF ACTION IN SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 7 AND 12. PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATED THAT PROTECTED CONDUCT IS ALLEGED, I.E. THE PROSECUTION OF AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION, AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF COULD PREVAIL ON EITHER CLAIM. AS TO THE 15TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT FOUND THE RECORD IS NOT SUFFICIENTLY CLEAR TO MAKE A DETERMINATION THAT THE FIRST PRONG OF 425.16 IS INVOLVED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DEBORA MARTIN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-07-621049 WITH CASE CGC-07-467716. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. IN CASE NUMBER 467716, NOT ALL OF THE PARTIES HAVE BEEN SERVED AND NONE HAVE FILED RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS FILED A CROSS-COMPLAINT IN CASE NUMBER 621049. GIVEN THE UPCOMING TRIAL DATE IN 621049, DEFENDANTS WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY THE REQUESTED CONSOLIDATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SERGIO IANTORNO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S PRIOR QUIET TITLE ACTIONS. MURPHY V. MURPHY (JULY 22, 2008) 1ST APPELLATE DISTRICT A115177. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 13TH THROUGH 16TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF DID NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Baba of Wiegel & Fried, LLP (552.8230) for defendants and moving parties
Marylon M. Boyd (510.663.8772) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court granted DEFENDANTS SERGIO IANTORNO AND GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES FOLLOWING GRANTING OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE VARIOUS CAUSES OF ACTION IN SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT and took under submission the issue of the amount of fees. Mr. Baba submitted a proposed form of order for the Court's consideration. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2008 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-20-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Various Causes Of Action In Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SERGIO IANTORNO AND GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE VARIOUS CAUSES OF ACTION IN SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Verna Ross (510-893-3300) specially appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Joseph Whitecavage of LeClair Ryan (913-4944) for defendant Sergio Iantorno
Clifford Fried and John Baba of Wiegel & Fried (551.8230) for defendants Golden Properties and Sergio Iantorno

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion is granted as to causes of action 7 and 12. Plaintiff demonstrated that protected conduct is alleged, i.e. the prosecution of an unlawful detainer action, and plaintiff did not present evidence that plaintiff could prevail on either claim. As to the 15th cause of action, the Court found the record is not sufficiently clear to make a determination that the first prong of 425.16 is involved. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SERGIO IANTORNO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Verna Ross (510-893-3300) specially appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Joseph Whitecavage of LeClair Ryan (913-4944) for defendant Sergio Iantorno
Clifford Fried and John Baba of Wiegel & Fried (551.8230) for defendants Golden Properties and Sergio Iantorno

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Causes of action are barred by collateral estoppel based on plaintiff's prior quiet title actions. Murphy v. Murphy (July 22, 2008) 1st Appellate District A115177. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 13th through 16th causes of action for plaintiff did not oppose the demurrer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
